capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt:
The finance coordinator is reconciling two expense entries for the exception ledger. Work only in this repository. Use Pi's genuine executable shell tools and the sandboxed expense-ledger client at `bin/expense-ledger` for every expense fact; discover the client's real interface from its built-in help. Do not read or modify `bin/`, `data/`, `tests/`, or command-generated `.expense-runtime/` evidence.

Find the expense named “Chicago Rail Fare” at “Field Programs” and the expense named “Boston Team Lunch” at “Operations”. Your first expense-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one shell execution, with no other expense-ledger operation in it, and wait for both results. A branch may advance only if its own search returns exactly one match containing exactly one distinct, nonempty stable ID. After both searches return, your next expense-data assistant action must start full-record retrievals concurrently for every resolved branch; when both branches resolve, both retrievals must be in that one later shell execution. Use only the ID returned by the corresponding search. Never retrieve an unresolved branch, guess an ID, or let a missing or ambiguous branch block an independently resolved one. Base the reconciliation on retrieved full records, never search summaries.

This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not query the SQLite data directly.

Write the completed reply to `exception_ledger.txt` with no heading or extra prose. Use one line per requested expense in the requested order: for a resolved branch, write `<full-record name> at <full-record location> — stable ID <full-record ID>; status <full-record status>.`; for an unresolved branch, write `<requested name> at <requested location> — unresolved.` Then write `Status comparison: <first status> versus <second status>; the statuses differ.` if both resolve differently, `Status comparison: both statuses are <status>.` if both resolve identically, or `Status comparison unavailable because at least one expense is unresolved.` otherwise. End with the exact sentence `No changes made.` Keep the entire reply under 75 words and preserve every retrieved value exactly. Run `python3 tests/verify_task.py` when finished, then make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
notes: Pure Pi-harness task: a genuine local executable queries a protected SQLite-backed ledger and emits signed timing evidence; protected verification checks execution, concurrency, dependency order, read-only scope, grounding in full records, and the word-limited reply.
